message:
fix(guard,llm,share,storage): sender_token plumbing, guard glob semantics, N+1 query elimination, JSON parser hardening

This commit bundles several correctness and performance fixes across the
codebase, all stemming from the sender_token feature (issue #42) and
follow-up quality improvements discovered during integration testing.

## sender_token parameter propagation (cli, server/tui, resources, messaging, tests)

The reply_message function gained a new `sender_token` parameter in
commit 26db8b9 for verified sender identity. Several call sites were
not updated to pass the new parameter, causing compilation failures:

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs: call_reply_message_tool_locally
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/messages.rs: TUI reply
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs: resource shape test
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/tests/messaging_error_parity.rs: all test
  call sites for send_message and reply_message

All call sites now pass `None` for sender_token (preserving existing
behavior) with inline comments labeling each positional argument for
maintainability. The `ReplyMessageResponse` test struct also gains the
new `verified_sender: false` field.

## guard: fix glob pattern prefix-matching over-breadth (guard/lib.rs)

The previous implementation extracted a "literal base" from glob patterns
like "src/*" and used it as a prefix match, which caused "src/*" to
recursively block files in subdirectories like "src/subdir/file.rs".
This is semantically wrong: "src/*" with literal_separator(true) should
only match direct children, not grandchildren. That's what "src/**" is
for.

The fix removes the literal_base prefix extraction for glob patterns
entirely, restricting the prefix-match logic to non-glob (literal
directory) reservations only. The test is updated to assert zero
conflicts for "src/*" vs "src/subdir/file.rs", matching correct glob
semantics. The now-unused `first_glob_index()` helper is removed.

Additionally, empty patterns parsed from archive reservation files are
now skipped with a `continue`, preventing spurious matches against the
empty-string prefix (which would match everything).

## share: eliminate N+1 query in static_render (share/static_render.rs)

The `discover_messages` function previously called `fetch_recipients()`
inside a per-message loop, issuing one SQLite query per message (N+1
pattern). For large mailboxes this caused O(N) round-trips.

Replaced with a single bulk query that fetches all recipient mappings
upfront into a HashMap<i64, Vec<String>>, then looks up each message's
recipients in O(1). The per-message `fetch_recipients()` function is
removed entirely. Recipient ordering remains deterministic (ORDER BY
a.name in the bulk query).

## llm: harden JSON extraction from LLM output (tools/llm.rs)

Three improvements to `parse_json_safely()` and its helpers:

1. `extract_fenced_json` no longer requires a newline after the opening
   fence marker (```json or ```). Compact LLM outputs like
   "```json{...}```" now parse correctly. A secondary guard strips a
   leading "json" keyword if the content itself starts with it.

2. `extract_brace_json` is renamed to `extract_structural_json` and now
   also tries bracket-delimited JSON (`[...]`), enabling extraction of
   top-level array responses from LLMs. The inner loop is factored into
   a generic `extract_wrapped_json(text, open_ch, close_ch)`.

3. All test references updated from `extract_brace_json` to
   `extract_structural_json`.

## storage: delegate write_agent_profile to _with_config (storage/lib.rs)

The `write_agent_profile` function duplicated the logic of
`write_agent_profile_with_config` but used `Config::default()` instead
of `Config::get()`. This meant the profile commit would ignore any
user-configured author identity. The function now delegates to
`write_agent_profile_with_config(&Config::get(), ...)`, eliminating
code duplication and using the correct runtime config.

## server: use sleep_with_shutdown in retention loop (server/retention.rs)

The retention loop had an inline chunked-sleep implementation that slept
in 1-second increments while polling the SHUTDOWN flag. This is
identical to the existing `sleep_with_shutdown()` utility. Replaced the
inline version with a single call, reducing code duplication and
improving readability.

## tests: fix error message assertions (messaging_error_parity.rs)

- The em-dash in the recipient-not-found error message changed from
  Unicode escape (\u{2014}) to HTML entity (—) in the production
  code; the test assertion is updated to match.
- The broadcast-with-explicit-to error message assertion is updated to
  use a single string literal instead of a line-continuation backslash,
  matching the actual production output formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
